name: Dual-Controller Competition with Pavlovian Override
description: A three-system architecture in which a goal-directed controller (model-based, hunger-sensitive) competes with a habitual controller (model-free Q-learning, hunger-insensitive) via a shifting arbitration weight, plus a Pavlovian cue-driven override that triggers eating whenever food is at the agent's location regardless of internal state. The goal-directed system computes action values analytically from current hunger and food positions. The habitual system learns cached Q-values using TD-learning with unmodulated food reward (making it devaluation-insensitive). Arbitration weight shifts from goal-directed to habitual as eating experience accumulates. A Pavlovian probability injects a fixed chance of eating whenever food is present. This captures the full Rangel (2013) three-controller framework (Postulate P5), devaluation insensitivity of habits, and cue-driven overeating (Postulates P5, P6).

variables.name: energy_store
variables.description: Normalized internal energy reserve. Depleted by basal metabolism each step, replenished by eating.

variables.name: hunger_drive
variables.description: Motivational drive state. Rises linearly each step during fasting and drops after eating. Directly modulates the goal-directed controller's food valuation.

variables.name: goal_directed_value
variables.description: Model-based action value computed from current hunger and expected outcomes. Sensitive to devaluation — when hunger is low, food value drops immediately.

variables.name: habitual_q_value
variables.description: Cached stimulus-response Q-value learned via TD-learning with UNMODULATED reward. Not sensitive to current hunger — represents 'what food was worth historically' regardless of current state.

variables.name: arbitration_weight
variables.description: Relative weight of the goal-directed controller in the value mixture. Starts high (novel situation → goal-directed dominance) and decays with eating experience (habitization). 1.0 = pure goal-directed, 0.0 = pure habitual.

variables.name: eating_experience_count
variables.description: Cumulative number of successful eat actions taken. Proxy for training repetitions that drive habitization of food-seeking behavior.

variables.name: pavlovian_override_probability
variables.description: Probability that the Pavlovian system overrides the computed policy with an automatic eat response when food is at the agent's cell. Fixed constant, not modulated by hunger — this is the key feature capturing cue-driven overeating.

variables.name: habitual_prediction_error
variables.description: Reward prediction error for the habitual system's Q-value update. Uses unmodulated reward, so the habitual system doesn't know about current hunger.

variables.name: ate_flag
variables.description: Binary indicator: 1 if the agent successfully consumed food on the previous step, 0 otherwise.

parameters.name: energy_gain_from_eating
parameters.source: Normalized; represents substantial meal (Jacquier 2016 energy intake scaling)

parameters.name: hunger_rise_rate
parameters.source: Adapted from Jacquier (2016) ghrelin pre-prandial rise dynamics

parameters.name: hunger_reduction_on_eating
parameters.source: Post-prandial satiation magnitude (Jacquier 2016: rapid ghrelin suppression)

parameters.name: habitual_learning_rate
parameters.source: Slower than typical RL learning rate; reflects gradual stimulus-response caching in dorsolateral striatum (Rangel 2013)

parameters.name: habitization_rate
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): habitual controller gains dominance with repeated experience. Rate tuned so transition occurs over ~100-400 eat events.

parameters.name: initial_goal_directed_weight
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): novel actions are dominated by goal-directed control; 0.8 = strong initial GD bias

parameters.name: pavlovian_override_strength
parameters.source: Rangel (2013): Pavlovian contribution is moderate but persistent; 20% override rate

parameters.name: step_cost
parameters.source: Small movement penalty to discourage aimless wandering

parameters.name: hunger_sensitivity
parameters.source: Sigmoid steepness for mapping energy deficit to hunger drive

rules.description: Energy dynamics: energy decreases by basal metabolic cost each step and increases when food is consumed.
Hunger dynamics: hunger rises linearly each step during fasting and drops sharply after eating, modeling short-term appetitive drive.
Goal-directed eat value: proportional to current hunger times base reward. Fully hunger-sensitive — a satiated agent (H ≈ 0) values eating at near zero.
Goal-directed move value: hunger-scaled food value discounted by distance to nearest food after the move, plus step cost.

Habitual Q-value update: TD-learning with UNMODULATED reward. The habitual system receives r_food regardless of hunger when eating, making it devaluation-insensitive. This is the key mechanistic distinction from the goal-directed controller.
rules.pseudocode:
R_H = r_food if ate == 1 else c_step
delta = R_H + gamma * max(Q_H[s_next, a'] for a' in actions) - Q_H[s_current, a_current]
Q_H[s_current, a_current] = Q_H[s_current, a_current] + alpha_Q * delta

rules.description: Controller arbitration: goal-directed weight decays linearly with cumulative eating experience, modeling habitization. After enough experience, the habitual controller dominates.
Integrated value: weighted mixture of goal-directed and habitual action values for each action.
rules.pseudocode: V_int_a = omega * V_GD_a + (1.0 - omega) * Q_H[s, a]

rules.description: Softmax base policy: probability of each action computed via softmax over integrated values.
rules.pseudocode: P_base(a) = exp(beta * V_int_a) / sum(exp(beta * V_int_j) for j in actions)

rules.description: Pavlovian override: when food is present at the agent's cell, a fixed probability p_pav causes the agent to eat regardless of computed values. This models cue-driven consummatory behavior that is NOT modulated by internal state.

decision_logic.description: Three-system hybrid decision process. The goal-directed controller computes action values from current hunger (devaluation-sensitive). The habitual controller retrieves cached Q-values learned with unmodulated reward (devaluation-insensitive). An arbitration weight mixes the two, shifting toward habitual control with experience. A Pavlovian override injects a fixed probability of eating whenever food is present, regardless of hunger or computed values. The agent samples from the final mixture policy.
pseudocode: # 1. Determine eating outcome from last step
ate = 1 if last_action_result indicates successful consumption else 0
food_at_cell = any(f for f in food_list if f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y)
dx_sign = sign(nearest.x - x) if nearest else 0
dy_sign = sign(nearest.y - y) if nearest else 0
# 4. Update habitual Q-values for PREVIOUS step (TD-learning with unmodulated reward)
R_H = r_food if ate == 1 else c_step
delta = R_H + gamma * max(Q_H[s, a'] for a' in actions) - Q_H[prev_s, prev_a]
# eat not meaningful if no food at cell
new_pos = apply_move((x, y), move_a, grid_width, grid_height)
# 7. Compute integrated values for each available action
# 8. Softmax base policy over integrated values
probs = {a: exp(beta * (v - max_v)) for a, v in V_int.items()}
P_base = {a: p / total for a, p in probs.items()}
# 9. Pavlovian override: if food is present, inject p_pav probability of eating
# 10. Sample action from final mixture policy
# 11. Store state and action for next step's Q-update

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.ate_flag: 1 if perception['last_action_result'] indicates eat success (resource consumed) else 0
perception_to_variables.food_at_cell: any(f for f in perception['resources']['food'] if f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'])

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect with env reward 1.0. Goal-directed controller uses hunger-modulated internal value (R3a). Habitual controller uses raw unmodulated r_food = 1.0 (R4). This asymmetry is the core mechanism for devaluation insensitivity.

expected_behaviors.description: Goal-directed devaluation sensitivity: early in training (high omega), a satiated agent (low H) avoids eating even when on food.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set n_eat = 0 (omega = 0.8), H = 0.05, place agent on food → assert P(eat) is low (< 0.3 excluding Pavlovian)

expected_behaviors.description: Habitual devaluation insensitivity: after extensive experience (low omega), agent eats food regardless of hunger state.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set n_eat = 400 (omega ≈ 0.0), H = 0.05, place agent on food → assert P_base(eat) is high (habitual Q drives eating)

expected_behaviors.description: Pavlovian override causes eating even when satiated and in goal-directed mode.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set n_eat = 0, H = 0.0, food at cell → assert P_final(eat) >= p_pav = 0.2 due to Pavlovian injection

expected_behaviors.description: Habitization over time: omega decreases as n_eat increases, shifting behavior from goal-directed to habitual.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: track omega over 500 steps with periodic eating → assert omega decreases monotonically

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger drives food seeking: when H is high, goal-directed values strongly favor moving toward food and eating.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H = 0.9, omega = 0.8, food at distance 3 → assert agent moves toward food (highest V_int for approach move)

expected_behaviors.description: Three-system mixture produces richer behavior than any single controller: agent occasionally eats when satiated (Pavlovian), approaches food efficiently (GD), and develops habitual food-seeking patterns over time.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 1000 steps → verify eating events occur across all hunger levels (not just high H); verify approach efficiency improves over time

expected_behaviors.description: Energy homeostasis maintained: energy oscillates around set-point as hunger drives eating cycles.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 500 steps → assert mean(E) is within ±0.2 of E_set

references: Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561
Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357
Jacquier, M. (2016). Mathematical modeling of the hormonal regulation of food intake and body weight. Doctoral thesis, Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1. HAL: tel-01273347
